summary: For an MVP/prototype the testing posture is broadly sound: §14 of the architecture draft and the REQ-T-001..006 block in REQUIREMENTS together cover the load-bearing invariants — cap enforcement (must "fail closed"), event-append/replay reconstruction, structured-output validation + repair/reject, critic/check schema validation, scoring, novelty, lineage rebuild, SSE resync, Langfuse correlation, and demo rehearsals. Most of the gaps the prompt probes for are actually present at least as one-liners, which is fine for this posture. The genuine gaps are: (1) RISK-014's contract-test suite for the freeze-first shared schemas is named in RISKS.md but has NO corresponding REQ-T requirement and is absent from the §14 test list, despite being the explicit mitigation for a "high" severity execution risk that drives the whole parallel-track plan; (2) "replay determinism" is asserted ("deterministic small run from seed through final summary") but the determinism contract is underspecified — there is no statement of WHAT must match on replay (byte-for-byte events vs. state-equivalent projection) given LLM nondeterminism, which makes REQ-T-001/REQ-T-003 untestable as written; (3) several items (secret-redaction tests, unsafe-check-rejection, prompt-injection fixtures) appear in the RISK register's "Test / Validation" column but are NOT mirrored into §14's must-have list or REQ-T, so they risk being dropped because §14 is what an implementer reads. None of these are deferred-with-flag; they are simply unmirrored. Recommend promoting the load-bearing safety tests (secret redaction, unsafe-check reject, prompt-injection) into §14 explicitly, adding a contract-test REQ, and nailing the replay determinism contract.

findings.finding: The contract-test suite for the freeze-first shared schemas — the explicit mitigation for RISK-014 (a 'high' severity execution risk) — is named only in the RISK register but has no REQ-T requirement and is absent from the §14 must-have test list. Since the entire parallel-track plan (§4A) depends on stable contracts, an unowned/unscheduled contract suite means schema drift across surfaces won't be caught by tests.
findings.evidence: RISKS.md RISK-014 row ('Freeze shared schemas/interfaces first; contract tests' / 'Contract test suite and architecture review'); absent from ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §14 must-have list and from REQUIREMENTS.md REQ-T-001..006
findings.proposed_fix: Add REQ-T-007 'Contract tests shall validate that each consumer of a shared schema (contracts package) agrees with the producer on event/command/review/check payload shapes' and add a '`contracts` package schema round-trip / consumer contract tests' bullet to §14. Tie it to the contracts package in §6 so it is owned.

findings.finding: Replay determinism is required but its CONTRACT is underspecified. REQ-T-001 says 'deterministic small run from seed through final summary' and REQ-T-003 says replay 'reconstructs dashboard-relevant state', but neither defines what must be deterministic given LLM nondeterminism — i.e. whether replay must reproduce events byte-for-byte from the stored log (replay = no fresh LLM calls, so deterministic) vs. whether a fresh seeded run must be reproducible (it cannot be, with live providers). Without this distinction the determinism test is unwriteable and could be authored to assert something impossible.
findings.evidence: REQUIREMENTS.md REQ-T-001, REQ-T-003; ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §14 ('event append/replay reconstruction'); REQ-F-014 'replay persisted runs without fresh LLM calls'
findings.proposed_fix: State explicitly that determinism applies to REPLAY of a persisted event log (no LLM calls -> bit-stable projection), not to fresh live runs. Define the determinism assertion as: 'projection rebuilt from the stored event log equals the projection captured at run end' (state-equivalence on a canonical serialization), and make the seed-to-summary fixture a recorded-event replay, not a live re-run.

findings.finding: Load-bearing SAFETY tests listed in the RISK register's Test/Validation column are not mirrored into §14's must-have list or REQ-T, so they may be dropped: secret redaction tests (RISK-009), unsafe-check-request rejection (RISK-010), and prompt-injection fixtures against critic/check prompts (RISK-008). These guard never-cut invariants (secrets never in prompts/events/traces; no arbitrary code execution), yet §14 — the doc an implementer reads — omits all three.
findings.evidence: RISKS.md RISK-008/009/010 Test columns ('Prompt-injection fixtures against critic/check prompts', 'Secret scanning / redaction tests', 'Unsafe check request should be rejected'); not present in ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §14 must-have list nor REQUIREMENTS.md REQ-T block
findings.proposed_fix: Add three bullets to §14 and matching REQ-T items: (a) secret-redaction test asserting no provider key appears in any persisted event/trace/UI payload; (b) check-runner allowlist test asserting a non-allowlisted/unsafe check request is rejected and recorded as skipped; (c) prompt-injection fixture asserting an injected candidate cannot move scoring policy, validation anchors, or trigger a tool the agenome lacks permission for.

findings.finding: Cap-enforcement is required to 'fail closed' (REQ-T-002) but the test list does not enumerate the kill-switch / operator-stop path as a tested behavior distinct from cap caps. RISK-003's validation pairs 'Cap-enforcement tests AND stop/kill smoke', and REQ-F-012 requires stop-preserves-partial-evidence, yet §14 folds 'time' into the generic cap bullet and lists no explicit stop/kill test.
findings.evidence: REQUIREMENTS.md REQ-T-002, REQ-F-012; RISKS.md RISK-003 ('Cap-enforcement tests and stop/kill smoke'); ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §14 ('cap enforcement for population/generation/energy/depth/tool/time')
findings.proposed_fix: Add an explicit '§14: operator-stop/kill-switch moves run to terminal state, halts spawning/model calls, and preserves replayable partial evidence' test bullet, separate from the population/energy cap tests, mapped to REQ-F-012.

findings.finding: Dashboard E2E / Playwright smoke is acknowledged but only as a risk-column mention (RISK-007) and not as a §14 must-have or a REQ-T. For an MVP a single happy-path Playwright smoke (start run -> see SSE events -> open final-idea evidence panel) is cheap insurance against the 'ornamental dashboard' risk, but as written it is unscheduled.
findings.evidence: RISKS.md RISK-007 ('Demo script review and Playwright smoke'); ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §14 lists SSE disconnect/reconnect smoke but no UI/Playwright E2E; no REQ-T for UI
findings.proposed_fix: Either add one §14 bullet for a single Playwright happy-path smoke (run start -> live event render -> final-idea evidence panel links resolve) OR explicitly flag UI E2E as deliberately deferred in favor of the SSE smoke + manual demo rehearsal. Recommend the former (one test) given showcase stakes.

findings.finding: The §14 must-have list and the REQUIREMENTS REQ-T table are not cross-referenced, so it is easy for one to drift from the other (the contract-test and safety-test gaps above are symptoms). §14 has 13 bullets; REQ-T has 6 rows; the mapping is implicit.
findings.evidence: ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §14 (13-bullet list, no REQ ids); REQUIREMENTS.md 'Testing Requirements' REQ-T-001..006
findings.proposed_fix: Annotate each §14 bullet with its REQ-T id (and add REQ-T rows for the contract/safety tests above) so the test list is traceable and the council/implementer can see coverage at a glance.

findings.finding: REQ-T-006 requires tests that correlate Doppl event IDs with Langfuse trace IDs, but Langfuse is non-authoritative and may be disabled in the demo (RISK-012). It is unclear whether this correlation test must pass in CI (requiring Langfuse credentials in CI) or is a manual smoke only.
findings.evidence: REQUIREMENTS.md REQ-T-006; RISKS.md RISK-012 ('Run with Langfuse disabled'); ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §14 ('Langfuse trace ID correlation smoke')
findings.proposed_fix: Decision needed: is trace-correlation a CI test or a manual rehearsal smoke? Recommended default: make it a manual/local smoke (not gating CI) and instead add a CI unit test that asserts the event payload CARRIES a trace-id field when tracing is enabled and degrades cleanly (no error) when Langfuse is disabled — avoids CI depending on Langfuse Cloud.
